mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to manage clusters across various infrastructures like AWS, Azure, or vSphere. The key trade-off is the complexity of setup versus the automation and consistency it provides for cluster lifecycle management.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Cluster API Helm repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs from the latest release.

steps.title: Install Cluster API components
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API components using Helm in the 'capi-system' namespace.

```bash

kubectl create namespace capi-system

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Creates the namespace for Cluster API.
> Installs the Cluster API components.

steps.title: Verify Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> This command lists all pods in the capi-system namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes Cluster API components and CRDs. Be aware that this may lead to data loss of any clusters managed by CAPI.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace capi-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> Uninstalls the Cluster API components.
> Removes the CRDs installed earlier.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure to backup your existing clusters. This step involves upgrading the Cluster API components and verifying the installation.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -A

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace capi-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n capi-system

```

> Backup your cluster state.
> Upgrades the Cluster API components to the specified version.
> Verifies that the upgraded components are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1alpha4"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD has not been installed or is not available.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml' to install the CRDs.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the Cluster API controller is not responding, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation for the CAPI pods and ensure they have sufficient CPU and memory.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'capi-system' namespace are in 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if there are misconfigurations in the Cluster API components.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n capi-system' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: failed to create control plane'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates issues with the infrastructure provider configuration.

**Fix:**
Verify the provider configurations and ensure that all required credentials and permissions are set correctly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers and improved error handling in the controller.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old v1alpha3 API version in favor of v1alpha4.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your existing Cluster resources to the new API version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n capi-system' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and have kubectl and Helm installed.
